0-25.label: Cold Trail
strings: The lounge holds its secrets tightly. Start with the people who were there.
Someone in this building is lying about something small that matters enormously.
A dead professor, five exhausted hackers, and a locked room. Time to wake them up.
The timeline is a mess of caffeine crashes and missing logic. Start connecting the dots.
First impressions are rarely the whole truth. Especially at 2 AM.

26-50.label: Something's Off
strings: The stories don't quite fit together yet, but the edges are starting to show.
At least one person in this room is pretending their memory is worse than it is.
There's a hole in the timeline. Find out what happened when nobody was looking.
The physical evidence doesn't match the verbal alibis. Keep shaking the tree.
Someone is acting far too calm for a person standing next to a dead judge.

51-75.label: You're Onto Something
strings: The contradictions are mounting. Someone's alibi has a hole in it.
The doctor's hesitation is telling. He knows more than he's saying about the cause of death.
You've broken through the first layer of lies. Now find the motive.
The scene was staged, but maybe not by the person who died.
Small, petty actions can have fatal consequences. Keep pulling the thread.

76-90.label: Getting Dangerous
strings: You're close. The truth is stranger than anyone has admitted.
Connect what Victor did, what Martha moved, and what Julian planned. The answer is in the overlap.
This wasn't a standard hit. It was a chain reaction of stupidity.
You have the pieces. Now you just need the exact sequence of events.
The organizer, the captain, the influencer. Everyone played a part, even if they didn't mean to.

91-100.label: You Know the Truth
strings: You have everything. The only question is how to explain the most absurd death in hackathon history.
Julian Byte tried to steal the show. The show had other ideas.
No mastermind. Just a perfect storm of petty collegiate grievance.
The puzzle is complete. Time to deliver the verdict before they all pass out.
It's all on the board. Make your accusation.
